report_name: Icelandic Firm Investigates Unprecedented AI-Assisted Cyberattack in Europe

attack_patterns.name: Develop Capabilities
attack_patterns.description: The attackers are hypothesized to have used a large AI model to analyze a vulnerability and devise a reliable exploit, overcoming complex prerequisites that previously deterred human attackers.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: A moderately severe vulnerability (CVSS 7.5) was weaponized to gain access and establish a foothold within the victim's environment, leading to persistent access.

attack_patterns.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
attack_patterns.description: The attack was engineered to create multiple, resilient pathways for persistence, suggesting the use of autostart execution mechanisms to maintain durable, long-term access.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol
attack_patterns.description: The attacker likely used subtle command-and-control channels, such as low-and-slow C2 communication over standard application protocols, to blend in with normal network traffic and evade detection.

vulnerabilities.name: Unspecified Vulnerability (CVSS 7.5)
vulnerabilities.description: A moderately severe vulnerability with a CVSS score of 7.5, previously considered difficult to exploit, was weaponized using AI assistance to gain persistent access to a European organization.

campaigns.name: AI-Assisted European Cyberattack
campaigns.description: A highly sophisticated cyberattack against a European entity, significant for its use of AI to develop a novel exploit for a moderately severe vulnerability, achieving resilient and persistent access.

identities.name: Syndis
identities.description: Icelandic cybersecurity firm that is investigating the novel AI-assisted cyberattack against one of its European clients.

identities.name: European client of Syndis
identities.description: The victim of a highly complex, AI-assisted cyberattack in Europe.

infrastructure.name: Low-and-slow C2 Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Subtle command-and-control infrastructure used for periodic, small data transfers to unknown domains, designed to blend in with normal network traffic.

indicators.name: Anomalous Process Tree Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Anomalous Process Tree Creation
id: 2a1b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects legitimate system processes spawning unusual child processes, which could indicate a novel in-memory exploit, as suggested in the analysis of an AI-assisted attack.
references:
  - https://www.ruv.is/english/2026-07-08-cyberattack-likely-assisted-by-ai-480804
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-08
level: medium
tags:
  - attack.t1055
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\svchost.exe'
      - '\lsass.exe'
      - '\wininit.exe'
      - '\services.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative scripts
  - Software installation and management tools
indicators.description: Detects legitimate system processes spawning unusual child processes, which could indicate a novel in-memory exploit, based on hunting hints from the incident analysis.
